reasoning: The base rate for riots in Curacao is vanishingly small; there have been 0 riots in the past 360 days leading up to June 2025. In order for the question to resolve positively, Curacao would need to experience 'more than ten times' the baseline value (which currently sits at 1.000). Because 10 * 1.000 = 10, there would need to be 11 or more 'Riot' events recorded by ACLED in a 30-day window. Our web searches regarding recent elections (March 2025, which were won definitively by the ruling party without unrest) and general unrest in Curacao in 2025 returned no signs of current or impending political crises, economic crashes, or natural disasters that would initiate an unprecedented wave of violence. Even during the peak of COVID-19 lockdown tensions in 2020, riots were short-lived. Thus, the probability of recording 11+ riots in the 30 days preceding any of the resolution dates is remarkably low. We assign the minimum allowable probability (0.02) to the nearest dates, gradually increasing the probability marginally up to 0.05 for December 2025 to reflect pure black swan/horizon uncertainty.
